svg.title: AI Student Assistant — Internal Execution Flow
svg.text: How the Code Executes — Step by Step
Creates AgentState dict with default values:
Reads entry_point="supervisor" → jumps to Supervisor node first
State object flows from node to node like a baton in a relay race
Calls Claude Haiku with current state summary
Prompt: "query=Summarise... has_rag=False has_tools=False → which agent next?"
Step A: takes query → HuggingFace model converts it to a 384-dim vector
Step B: sends vector to ChromaDB → cosine similarity search across 9 stored chunks
Step C: returns 4 most similar text chunks from biology_notes.pdf
Step D: formats them: "[Chunk 1 | biology_notes.pdf | Page 1]\n Cell is the basic unit..."
Step E: writes to state["rag_context"] = that formatted text
Prompt now: "has_rag=True has_tools=False has_answer=False → which agent next?"
Haiku sees rag_context is filled → replies: "answer_agent"
Step A: reads state["rag_context"] (the 4 chunks) + state["tool_results"] (empty)
Step B: fills ANSWER_PROMPT template with query + rag_context
Step C: sends filled prompt to Claude Sonnet via LangChain LCEL chain
Step D: writes result to state["final_answer"] and state["next_agent"]="END"
answer_agent.py → chains/answer_chain.py → chains/prompts.py → Claude Sonnet API
Skips LLM call entirely → sets next_agent = "END"
Conditional edge hits "END" → LangGraph stops the loop
